children.label: A
children.text: social, health, welfare, and educational services (including literacy tutoring), legal and other counseling services and assistance, including tax counseling and assistance and financial counseling, and library, recreational, and other similar services;

children.label: B
children.text: conservation, maintenance, or restoration of natural resources;

children.label: C
children.text: community betterment or beautification;

children.label: D
children.text: antipollution and environmental quality efforts;

children.label: E
children.text: weatherization activities;

children.label: F
children.text: economic development; and

children.label: G
children.text: such other services essential and necessary to the community as the Secretary determines by rule to be appropriate.

children.heading: Community service employment
children.label: 2 Community service employment
children.text: The term “community service employment” means part-time, temporary employment paid with grant funds in projects described in , through which eligible individuals are engaged in community service and receive work experience and job skills that can lead to unsubsidized employment.section 3056(b)(1)(D) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “eligible individual” means an individual who is age 55 or older and who has a low income (including any such individual whose income is not more than 125 percent of the poverty line), excluding any income that is unemployment compensation, a benefit received under title XVI of the Social Security Act ( et seq.), a payment made to or on behalf of veterans or former members of the Armed Forces under the laws administered by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, or 25 percent of a benefit received under title II of the Social Security Act ( et seq.), subject to subsection (b).42 U.S.C. 138142 U.S.C. 401

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: i Exclusion
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this paragraph, the term “eligible individual” does not include an individual who has participated in projects under this subchapter for a period of 48 months in the aggregate (whether or not consecutive) after , unless the period was increased as described in clause (ii).July 1, 2007

children.label: I
children.text: have a severe disability;

children.label: II
children.text: are frail or are age 75 or older;

children.label: III
children.text: meet the eligibility requirements related to age for, but do not receive, benefits under title II of the Social Security Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 401

children.label: IV
children.text: live in an area with persistent unemployment and are individuals with severely limited employment prospects;

children.label: V
children.text: have limited English proficiency or low literacy skills; or

children.label: VI
children.text: have been incarcerated within the last 5 years or are under supervision following release from prison or jail within the last 5 years.

children.heading: Income
children.label: 4 Income
children.text: In this section, the term “income” means income received during the 12-month period (or, at the option of the grantee involved, the annualized income for the 6-month period) ending on the date an eligible individual submits an application to participate in a project carried out under this subchapter by such grantee.

children.heading: Local workforce development board; state workforce development board
children.label: 5 Local workforce development board; state workforce development board
children.text: The terms “local workforce development board” and “State workforce development board” have the meanings given the terms “local board” and “State board”, respectively, in section 3 of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act ().29 U.S.C. 3102

children.heading: Pacific Island and Asian Americans
children.label: 6 Pacific Island and Asian Americans
children.text: The term “Pacific Island and Asian Americans” means Americans having origins in any of the original peoples of the Far East, Southeast Asia, the Indian Subcontinent, or the Pacific Islands.

children.heading: Program
children.label: 7 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the older American community service employment program established under this subchapter.

children.heading: Supportive services
children.label: 8 Supportive services
children.text: The term “supportive services” means services, such as transportation, child care, dependent care, housing, and needs-related payments, that are necessary to enable an individual to participate in activities authorized under this subchapter, consistent with the provisions of this subchapter.

children.heading: Unemployed
children.label: 9 Unemployed
children.text: The term “unemployed”, used with respect to a person or individual, means an individual who is without a job and who wants and is available for work, including an individual who may have occasional employment that does not result in a constant source of income.
is 65 years of age or older; or

children.label: A
children.text: has a disability;

children.label: B
children.text: has limited English proficiency or low literacy skills;

children.label: C
children.text: resides in a rural area;

children.label: D
children.text: is a veteran;

children.label: E
children.text: has low employment prospects;

children.label: F
children.text: has failed to find employment after utilizing services provided under title I of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act [ et seq.];29 U.S.C. 3111

children.label: G
children.text: is homeless or at risk for homelessness; or

children.label: H
children.text: has been incarcerated within the last 5 years or is under supervision following release from prison or jail within the last 5 years.
